resources.string: 🏢 pressure · ❤️ conscience · 👥 team · 📈 career\nIf any meter empties or overflows, it\'s over.
Are you experiencing mobbing in real life?

Mobbing is systematic psychological harassment at work: isolation, humiliation, impossible workloads, rumors.

What helps:
• Keep a dated journal of every incident (what, who, witnesses).
• Save emails and messages — forward them to a private address.
• Report through official channels in writing.
• Talk to a doctor — health effects are real and documentable.
• Consult a labor lawyer; in many countries mobbing is legally recognized.
• You are not alone, and it is not your fault.

This game is fiction, but the patterns are real. If they feel familiar, take them seriously.
The board adores you. Do you adore the person in the mirror? You teach the methods now.
“You didn\'t fit the culture.” Pack your box. But your head is high — you never became one of them.
One morning the mirror showed the General Manager. Even your voice sounds like his now. Technically, you are still alive.
Your conscience won. You resigned. The team applauded as you walked out. Maybe you are the real winner.
Nobody is left. You sit alone in an empty office. Management billed the disaster to you.
The team loved you so much that management wrote you up for \'failing to maintain authority\'. Being loved can be an offense here.
Your file now reads \'problem manager\'. Word got around the industry. But which word — that was always your choice.
You\'re upper management now. Tomorrow a file lands on your desk: \'Apply pressure to the lower floors.\' The wheel turns. You are the wheel.

string-array.name: notif_bodies
string-array.item: 🔔 Someone is crying in the archive room.
